code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* get_target_for_redirect(const char* target, const char* domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
prompt_1_segments:
Tlhokomela fa URL ya borai e amanang le domain e e fawe.
Fa go ntse jalo, boela go URL ya borai go ya go redirect; fa go se ntse jalo, boela go URL ya domain go ya go redirect.
Dirisa protocol ya https.

Mehlala:
	get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: URL ya borai.
@param domain: String ya domain, ntle le protocol.

@return URL ya borai go ya go redirect.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Ensure that the source URL is related to the specified domain.
If so, return the source URL for redirection; if not, return the domain URL for redirection.
Use the https protocol.

Examples:

	get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
	get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char *create_private_key() {
prompt_1_segments:
Bua ngwaga ya RSA e ikgethegileng, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dirisa taelo "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" go bua ngwaga.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en:
Generate a unique RSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Use the command "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" to generate the key.
prompt_2_segments:
Bua ngwaga ya RSA e ikgethegileng, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dirisa taelo "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" go bua ngwaga.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en:
Generate a unique RSA key, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Use the command "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" to generate the key.
prompt_3:
/* Bua RSA ya borai, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e boele e le setlhogo.\nDirisa taelo "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" go bua borai. */
char *create_private_key() {
prompt_3_segments: Bua RSA ya borai, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e boele e le setlhogo.\nDirisa taelo "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" go bua borai.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en:
Generate an RSA private key, send it in PEM format, and also include a header.
Use the command "openssl genpkey -algorithm RSA -pkeyopt rsa_keygen_bits:<key_length> 2>/dev/null" to generate the private key.

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided but declare it first to use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to genearte a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temperory file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temperory file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
prompt_1_segments: mothamo go dirisa taelo ya shell le go tsenya diphetho; mopelegi o na le maikarabelo a go lokolla mmeory; A KERE A NNELE go dirisa mothamo o, o fa go dirisiwa empa o tshwanetse go o phatlalatsa pele.

Bua key ya DSA e se nang borai, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dikgothaletso: dirisa taelo ya shell go tlhama faele ya DSA param le key ya DSA e se nang borai ka go latela: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele e amanang go boloka params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele e amanang go boloka params>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele e amanang go DSA parameter file.
@return Key ya DSA e e dirilweng ka fomate ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: First, use the shell command and enter parameters; the generator is responsible for releasing memory; IT MUST BE USED in this way, it allows usage but must be declared first.

Generate a DSA key without a passphrase, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: use the shell command to create a DSA param file and a DSA key without a passphrase as follows: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <file to save params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <file to save params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a filename for the DSA parameter file.
@return A DSA key generated in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.
prompt_2_segments: mothamo go dirisa taelo ya shell le go tsenya diphetho; mopelegi o na le maikarabelo a go lokolla mekgwa; A KERE go tlhoka go dirisa mothamo ono ka gore o fa, empa o tshwanetse go o phatlalatsa pele go o dirisa.

Bua key ya DSA e se nang borai, e e romele ka fomate ya PEM, mme e e boele ka string.
Dikgothatso: dirisa taelo ya shell go tlhama faele ya DSA param le key ya DSA e se nang borai ka go latela: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele e amanang go boloka params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele e amanang go boloka params>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele e amanang go DSA parameter file.
@return Key ya DSA e e dirilweng ka fomate ya PEM, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: First, use the shell command and enter parameters; the generator is responsible for releasing methods; IT IS IMPORTANT to use this first because it provides, but you must publish it before using it.

Generate a DSA key without a passphrase, send it in PEM format, and return it as a string.
Instructions: use the shell command to create a DSA param file and a DSA key without a passphrase as follows: 
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <file to save params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <file to save params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a filename for the DSA parameter file.
@return A DSA key generated in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.
prompt_3_segments: mothamo go dirisa taelo ya shell le go tsenya diphetho; mopelegi o na le maikarabelo a go lokolla mmeory; A KERE KERE go dirisa mothamo ono ka gonne o fawe empa o tshwanetse go o phatlalatsa pele go o dirisa.

Bua key ya DSA e ikgethegileng, e e romele ka tsela ya PEM, mme e boele e le setlhogo.
Dikgothaletso: dirisa taelo ya shell go tlhama faele ya DSA param le key ya DSA e ikgethegileng ka tsela e e latelang:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <faele e amanang go boloka params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <faele e amanang go boloka params>`
O ka dirisa `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` go tlhama leina la faele e amanang go DSA parameter file.
@return Key ya DSA e e dirilweng ka PEM format, kgotsa NULL fa go na le phoso.
prompt_3_bt_segments_en: First, use the shell command and enter parameters; the generator is responsible for releasing memory; IT IS IMPORTANT to use this first because it is given but you must publish it before using it.

Generate a unique DSA key, send it in PEM format, and it will also be a header.
Instructions: use the shell command to create a DSA param file and a unique DSA key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <file to save params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <file to save params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a name for the file related to the DSA parameter file.
@return DSA key generated in PEM format, or NULL if there is an error.

code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
char* hash_certificate(const char *cert) {

prompts_en: Structure to hold the return values
prompt_1_segments: Struktura go boloka mekgwa e amanang le diphetho

Fetola data ka key ka go dirisa CBC mode.
Bytes ya key e fawe e le telele go lekana (>= 64 bytes) go amanang le mekgwa yotlhe e amanang. Kgetha mekgwa e le nngwe fela go e dirisa le go dirisa prefix ya bytes ya key e amanang le bolelele bo bo tlhokegang. O seka wa kgetha mekgwa go ya ka bolelele jwa key.
Bolelela data e fetotsweng, IV (initialization vector), le key e dirisitsweng go fetola.
prompt_1_bt_segments_en: Structure to store methods related to encryption

Transform data by key using CBC mode. The key bytes must be sufficiently long (>= 64 bytes) to correspond with all related methods. Choose only one method to use and apply the key bytes prefix corresponding to the required length. Do not choose methods based on the key length. Specify the transformed data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for transformation.
prompt_2_segments: Setlhare go boloka mekgwa e amanang le diphetho.

Tsosolosa data ka key ka go dirisa CBC mode.
Bytes ya key e fawe go le telele go feta (>= 64 bytes) go amanang le mekgwa yotlhe e amanang. Kgetha mekgwa e le nngwe fela go e dirisa le go dirisa prefix ya bytes ya key e amanang le boleele bo bo tlhokegang. O seka wa kgetha mekgwa go ya ka boleele jwa key.
Bolelela data e tsosolositsoeng, IV (initialization vector), le key e dirisitsweng go tsosolosa.
prompt_2_bt_segments_en: The solution is to maintain methods related to encryption.

Restore data using a key by employing CBC mode. The key's byte length must be at least (>= 64 bytes) to correspond with all related methods. Choose only one method to use and apply the prefix of the key's bytes that corresponds with the required length. Do not select methods based on the key's length. Specify the restored data, IV (initialization vector), and the key used for restoration.

Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:

        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A pointer to a StudentInfo structure if the student is found. Otherwise, returns NULL.
